item.title: What Stock Traders Can Expect From SpaceX’s Trading Debut
item.description: Investors will soon have a sense of how SpaceX shares will perform after the largest initial public offering ever — but they may need to wait several hours to get in on the trading action.

item.title: Ken Leech to Plead Guilty in US ‘Cherry Picking’ Fraud Case
item.description: Ken Leech, the former co-chief investment officer at Western Asset Management Co., will plead guilty in a case related to allegations that he fraudulently assigned winning trades to favored clients while forcing others to take losses, days before he was to face a federal criminal trial.

item.title: From London to Sydney, Traders Ready to Watch SpaceX’s Debut
item.description: Initial public offerings don’t normally keep investors awake on the other side of the globe, but SpaceX’s record-breaking IPO is far from ordinary.

item.title: France’s Mistral in Funding Talks at About €20 Billion Valuation
item.description: French startup Mistral AI is in talks to raise around €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a valuation of roughly €20 billion, according to people familiar with the discussions, providing Europe’s artificial intelligence champion with a cash injection as it competes in a costly computing race against competitors in the US and China.

item.title: Air India Crash Report Delayed as Investigation Continues
item.description: The government agency probing last year’s Air India crash has delayed its final report, saying the investigation into the country’s worst commercial airline disaster isn’t finished yet.

item.title: Morocco Weighs China Free-Trade Agreement, Minister Says
item.description: China asked Morocco to sign a free trade agreement, the North African nation’s industry minister said, as Rabat weighs whether closer economic ties with Beijing would benefit its export-oriented manufacturing sector.

item.title: IMF Said to Agree on Kyiv Loan Deal Despite Missed Condition
item.description: The International Monetary Fund agreed to release its next tranche of a loan to Ukraine, despite Kyiv’s failure to meet a key condition as the war-weary nation faces a growing backlash against the requirements.

item.title: Gary Gensler Says Kalshi Is Flat Wrong: Sports Bets Aren’t Swaps
item.description: Gary Gensler, overseer of Wall Street for significant parts of the last two decades, has a message for anyone arguing sports betting is a matter for federal financial regulators: They’re wrong.

item.title: Leveraged ETFs Aren’t Just a Korea Problem
item.description: They’re growing in the US too.

item.title: Iran Is Under Mounting Strain From US Naval Blockade
item.description: With ships trapped and oil revenue falling, Tehran has some tough choices ahead.

item.title: Buy Toll, Sell Lennar as Rich Homebuyers Outpace First-Timers
item.description: An economy that’s been good for the rich makes shares of high-end homebuilders a better bet than those focused on entry-level buyers, according to one Wall Street analyst.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Jumps Over Target Limit Before Rate Meeting
item.description: Brazil’s annual inflation climbed over the upper bound of the target range in May, complicating central bankers ability to deliver additional interest rate cuts when they gather next week.

item.title: India Shifts Into Crisis Mode With Fuel Curbs, Wider Deficit
item.description: India is ramping up its economic defenses as the war in Iran drives up energy costs, prompting authorities to ration diesel, weigh spending cuts and hunt for overseas capital.

item.title: SpaceX to Begin Trading After Record $75 Billion IPO
item.description: SpaceX to Begin Trading After Record $75 Billion IPO【原文：SpaceX to Begin Trading After Record $75 Billion IPO】

item.title: Poland’s Dabrowski Floats 2026 Rate Cut View as Price Risks Fade
item.description: Polish central bankers are increasingly optimistic they can avoid interest-rate hikes as soft inflation creates a chance for easing this year, according to Monetary Policy Council member Ireneusz Dabrowski.

item.title: Singapore to Implement Revised Rules for Single Family Offices
item.description: Singapore’s central bank will roll out a revised framework for single family offices on June 15, making it easier for wealthy families to set up investment entities in the city-state while strengthening defenses against money laundering.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 12, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.6% as of 7:36 a.m. in New York following fresh evidence that the US and Iran are nearing a provisional agreement to end their war. SpaceX made history with a $75 billion IPO that instantly turned it into one of the biggest public companies in the world.

item.title: US Futures Rally With All Eyes on SpaceX, Iran War Peace Hopes
item.description: US futures climbed ahead of the open on Friday, propelled by optimism about pending trading in SpaceX, which made history with the biggest-ever IPO, and the potential for an interim peace deal in the Iran conflict.

item.title: Vanguard Ends BlackRock’s 20-Year Run Atop US ETF Market
item.description: Vanguard Group has overtaken BlackRock Inc. as the largest exchange-traded fund issuer, a seismic shift at the top of the $15.2 trillion US industry.

item.title: ‘Extreme’ Rotations Leave Stock Market Bulls Without a Playbook
item.description: Investors used to watching US equities go nowhere but up since late March have spent the past week without a reliable playbook.

item.title: Ukraine Steel Giant Tests Investor Demand After Bond Redemption
item.description: Metinvest BV is sounding out fixed-income investors on a potential return to the market after the Ukrainian steel and mining group had to fund the redemption of a bond using cash earlier this year.

item.title: Jakarta Plans Indonesia’s First Municipal Bond Within a Year
item.description: Jakarta aims to issue Indonesia’s first municipal bond within a year as part of efforts to diversify city revenue sources following budget cuts.

item.title: Fortress-Backed Poundstretcher Wins Court Approval for Rent Cuts
item.description: Poundstretcher Ltd.’s restructuring plan won court approval, giving the struggling UK discount retailer a lifeline to continue trading as it battles deteriorating finances and a costly portfolio of stores.

item.title: Tether’s Brief Flip of Ether in Value Gives Crypto a Reality Check
item.description: For a few hours last weekend, Tether’s USDT stablecoin became more valuable than Ether.

item.title: Latest AI Doomers Imagine How Europe ‘Slides Into Irrelevance’
item.description: Report urges ‘most ambitious’ effort to keep pace in the AI race

item.title: ECB Stands Out With First G7 Rate Hike Since Start of Iran War
item.description: The problem with doing your own thing is that you look more exposed if you’re wrong.

item.title: Credit Traders Unwind a $20 Billion Wartime Short in Europe
item.description: Credit investors have given up their wartime hedges on European companies.

item.title: UK Power Prices Turn Negative as Renewables Set to Flood Grid
item.description: UK power prices turned negative for the first time since late April as strong wind and solar generation are set to flood the grid.

item.title: ENN Natural Gas Ditches Restructuring Plan, $12 Billion Buyout
item.description: ENN Natural Gas Co.’s board terminated a planned restructuring, including a nearly $12 billion buyout offer for ENN Energy Holdings Ltd. and a second listing in Hong Kong.

item.title: India Inflation Accelerates to 3.93%, Stays Below RBI Target
item.description: India’s inflation accelerated in May but remained below the Reserve Bank of India’s 4% target, even as higher fuel costs stemming from disruptions in global energy markets added to price pressures.
